source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the standard FSAE / Formula Student IA architecture: a crushable attenuator body (aluminum honeycomb as specified in the original sample), a steel anti-intrusion plate (AIP) at the rear face, 4-6 mounting tabs that tie the AIP to the Front Bulkhead via M8 Grade 8.8 bolts, and a test-rig interface plate for the rigid-barrier impact certification. The AIP, bolts, pattern, materials, and energy/deceleration thresholds are all directly anchored to the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic Impact Attenuator (IA) for a student-built open-wheel race vehicle. Per the governing structural demonstration standard, the IA must absorb at least 7350 J of energy when struck by a 300 kg mass at 7 m/s into a solid non-yielding barrier (equivalent impact test); the mean deceleration of the 300 kg test mass must not exceed 20 g, and the peak deceleration must not exceed 40 g. The IA mounts ahead of the Front Bulkhead and must completely cover the bulkhead plate. Geometric constraints: minimum length 200 mm (along the longitudinal axis), minimum height 200 mm, minimum width 100 mm. Rear face (mounting face) of the IA shall be at least as large as the Front Bulkhead opening (typically 200 mm x 100 mm). Anti-intrusion plate (AIP) is a 1.5 mm (0.060 in) steel plate, fully covers the rear of the IA, bolted to the Front Bulkhead with 4x M8 Grade 8.8 bolts on a 150 mm x 80 mm pattern. Material: aluminum honeycomb core (3.1 pcf / 50 kg/m^3) or foam or crushable-tube construction; if honeycomb, cell size 9.5 mm, density 50 kg/m^3, bare compressive crush strength 689 kPa. Test-assembly certificate shall state IA + AIP must together absorb >= 7350 J under the specified impact. Frame tubes (context for the interface only; the IA itself is separately qualified): Front Bulkhead 25.4 mm OD x 2.4 mm wall mild steel. Load/test case: (R1) Dynamic impact 300 kg x 7 m/s into rigid barrier; energy absorption >= 7350 J. (R2) Mean deceleration of 300 kg test mass <= 20 g. (R3) Peak deceleration of 300 kg test mass <= 40 g. (R4) After impact, no penetration of the AIP (no part of the IA may penetrate the plane of the Front Bulkhead by more than 25 mm). (R5) IA mass (core + skin, excluding AIP) <= 3.0 kg. (R6) Quasi-static equivalent check: at 20 g x 300 kg = 58,860 N sustained load, max stress in any Front Bulkhead tube <= yield (305 MPa for 1010 steel). Primary verification: explicit-dynamics FEA OR an equivalent quasi-static energy-absorption FEA with documented equivalence. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 IA + AIP subassembly, test report or FEA output, and a report listing energy absorbed in J, mean g, peak g, IA mass.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The deliverable is reconfigured as the IA + AIP subassembly that the original brief already names. Parts: the crushable attenuator body (aluminum honeycomb core per the original sample), the 1.5 mm steel anti-intrusion plate (AIP) that fully covers the rear face, 4-6 mounting tabs welded or bonded to the AIP that accept the 4x M8 Grade 8.8 bolts on the 150 x 80 mm pattern into the Front Bulkhead, and a test-rig interface plate for the rigid-barrier impact certification. FEM verification runs on the assembled IA + AIP only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. The 4x M8 Grade 8.8 bolt DCR must be <= 1.0 under LC2 quasi-static equivalent (58,860 N). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim.
short_prompt:
Design an Impact Attenuator (IA) + Anti-Intrusion Plate (AIP) subassembly for a student-built open-wheel race vehicle. Per the governing structural demonstration, the IA must absorb >= 7350 J when struck by a 300 kg mass at 7 m/s into a solid non-yielding barrier; mean deceleration of the 300 kg test mass <= 20 g, peak deceleration <= 40 g.

The IA mounts ahead of the Front Bulkhead and fully covers the bulkhead plate. Minimum length 200 mm, minimum height 200 mm, minimum width 100 mm; rear face at least as large as the Front Bulkhead opening (typically 200 x 100 mm). AIP is 1.5 mm (0.060 in) steel plate bolted to the Front Bulkhead with 4x M8 Grade 8.8 bolts on a 150 x 80 mm pattern.

Core: aluminum honeycomb 3.1 pcf (50 kg/m^3), cell size 9.5 mm, bare compressive crush strength 689 kPa. Front Bulkhead tube (context): 25.4 mm OD x 2.4 mm wall mild steel.

LC1 dynamic: 300 kg x 7 m/s; energy absorption >= 7350 J. LC2 quasi-static equivalent: 20 g x 300 kg = 58,860 N; max stress in any Front Bulkhead tube <= yield (305 MPa for 1010 steel). AIP penetration past Front Bulkhead plane <= 25 mm. IA mass (excluding AIP) <= 3.0 kg.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 7 m/s barrier impact is formally nonlinear transient dynamics with crushable core material.

prompt.title: Student race vehicle Impact Attenuator + AIP assembly
prompt.brief: IA + AIP subassembly: honeycomb core attenuator body + 1.5 mm steel anti-intrusion plate + 4-6 mounting tabs + test-rig interface plate. Absorbs >= 7350 J at 300 kg x 7 m/s, mean g <= 20, peak g <= 40, IA mass <= 3 kg. FEM verification at assembled level.

geometric_constraints.IA_rear_face_coverage: at least as large as Front Bulkhead opening (typically 200 mm x 100 mm)

boundary_conditions.mounting: IA rear face bolted to Front Bulkhead plate via AIP + 4x M8 Grade 8.8 on 150 x 80 mm pattern
boundary_conditions.impact: 300 kg rigid mass travelling at 7 m/s into IA front face

objective.constraint: meet all deceleration and energy targets

parts.name: attenuator_body_core
parts.role: crushable aluminum-honeycomb block that absorbs the 7350 J barrier impact by progressive cell crushing; the primary energy-absorption element

geometric_constraints.rear_face_coverage: fully covers the AIP rear face

parts.name: anti_intrusion_plate_AIP
parts.role: 1.5 mm steel plate bonded / welded to the rear face of the attenuator body; prevents barrier penetration into the Front Bulkhead plane beyond 25 mm (R4) and distributes the impact load to the 4x M8 mounting pattern

geometric_constraints.plan_dimensions_mm: at least 200 x 100 (covers Front Bulkhead opening)

parts.name: mounting_tab
parts.role: tab welded to the AIP that captures the M8 Grade 8.8 bolt head or nut; one tab per bolt location; carries the bolt DCR checked under LC2
parts.count_note: Minimum 4 (one per M8 bolt in the 150 x 80 mm pattern); teams often add 2 extra tabs for side-load attenuation.

parts.name: test_rig_interface_plate
parts.role: flat interface plate mounted to the test-rig side during the 300 kg x 7 m/s rigid-barrier certification; not part of the flight / race hardware but required for the equivalent-impact test configuration described in the original prompt

geometric_constraints.test_bolt_pattern: matches rig-side 4x M12 on 200 x 200 mm

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X = longitudinal (impact direction), Y = vehicle transverse, Z = vertical

pass_fail_criteria.description: 4x M8 Grade 8.8 AIP-to-Front-Bulkhead bolt DCR shall be <= 1.0 under LC2 quasi-static equivalent (58,860 N distributed over the 150 x 80 mm pattern).
Attenuator-body-to-AIP bond shear DCR shall be <= 1.0 under LC1 impact; the bond must not separate before the honeycomb has absorbed >= 7350 J.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: solid hex / honeycomb microstructure OR homogenized crushable foam for the core; shell for AIP and tabs
analysis_types: nonlinear explicit dynamics (LC1) on assembled IA + AIP
quasi-static equivalent (LC2) on assembled IA + AIP

interfaces.mediated_by: structural adhesive bond + optional mechanical fasteners around the perimeter
interfaces.load_path: axial crushing force from the core into the AIP during LC1; bond must not separate before 7350 J is absorbed

interfaces.mediated_by: all-around fillet weld at each tab
interfaces.load_path: impact reaction from AIP into mounting tab, then into M8 bolt

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M8 Grade 8.8 bolts on 150 x 80 mm pattern
interfaces.load_path: full 58,860 N LC2 quasi-static equivalent load into the Front Bulkhead tubes (checked by R6 and R_asm1)

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M12 bolts on 200 x 200 mm rig-side pattern
interfaces.load_path: barrier reaction during the 300 kg x 7 m/s certification impact; load path is used only during the certification test, not during vehicle service

fem_check_policy.rationale: The IA certification is defined on the assembled IA + AIP: the energy-absorption, mean-g, peak-g, and penetration pass/fail criteria all characterize the subassembly response under a single impact event. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: The original prompt already names the IA + AIP subassembly (STEP AP242 deliverable is 'IA + AIP subassembly') and defines the 4x M8 Grade 8.8 bolt interface into the Front Bulkhead; a faithful representation requires the attenuator body, the AIP, the mounting tabs that accept the bolts, and a test-rig interface plate for the certification configuration.
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact mounting-tab count (>= 4), test-rig interface plate dimensions, and bond detail are speculative; AIP thickness, bolt pattern, honeycomb specs, and impact / deceleration limits are anchored to the original sample.
